mnemonics.title: Remember Jump Instructions
mnemonics.content: JUMP: JZ, JNZ, JC, JNC - Jump if Zero, Not Zero, Carry, No Carry. Use: Decision making based on flags/accumulator.

mnemonics.title: DJNZ Loop Pattern
mnemonics.content:
MOV R0, #10h ; Load counter
LOOP: ... ; Body
 DJNZ R0, LOOP ; Decrement, jump if not zero
 ; Continues here when R0 = 0

mnemonics.title: CALL-RETURN Pair
mnemonics.content: CALL instructions (LCALL/ACALL) push PC+3/PC+2 onto stack. RET/RETI pop it back to continue execution.

acronyms.usage: Unconditional jump to any address in 64KB memory, 3 bytes opcode+address
Unconditional relative jump within -128 to +127 bytes, 2 bytes

acronyms.expansion: Jump if Zero / Jump if Not Zero
acronyms.usage: Test accumulator content for branching decisions

acronyms.expansion: Jump if Carry / Jump if No Carry
acronyms.usage: Test carry flag after arithmetic operations

acronyms.expansion: Decrement and Jump if Not Zero
acronyms.usage: Implement loop counters - decrement register, branch if result ≠ 0

acronyms.expansion: Compare and Jump if Not Equal
acronyms.usage: Compare two values and branch if unequal, also sets Carry flag
Call subroutine anywhere in 64KB program memory, pushes return address
Call subroutine within 2KB of current PC, more compact than LCALL
Return from ISR, restores interrupt enable flip-flop

visualTips.visualization: Think of SJMP as standing on current instruction and jumping forward/backward up to 127 steps (like -128 to +127 stairs). LJMP can reach any floor in the 64-story building (64KB).
When CALL is executed, imagine two numbers (return address) being pushed onto a stack - like placing a bookmark, then using it to find your place when RET is called.
Picture a countdown timer: Start with N, each DJNZ decrements by 1. When it reaches 0, exit loop; otherwise, jump back to continue.

analogies.analogy: Like a GPS that recalculates route only when you take a wrong turn. The 8051 checks the condition (like checking road signs) and decides whether to take the alternate path or continue straight.
Think of a library book return system: CALL is like checking out a book (saving your place), RET is returning to your original page by using the bookmark saved on the stack.
Like comparing two students' scores: If first score < second, Carry flag raised (student behind). If first >= second, Carry cleared (student ahead or equal).
